text: A bucket list
To set your sights on
To make the most of
On the horizon
Long-overdue
An aspiration
To broaden your horizons
To seize the moment
A fresh start
Uncharted
To map out
Attainable
To look forward to
By next year, I will have reached my goal.
This time next year, I will be travelling.
By December, I will have finished the course.
At noon tomorrow, we will be flying to Lisbon.
By next spring, she will have walked the trail.
So, Pricila, what is the one big goal you have set your sights on for the year ahead?
A long-overdue trip across Portugal. By next autumn, I will have walked part of the coast, it is right at the top of my bucket list.
Wonderful. So this time next year, what will you be doing?
I will be exploring places that are still uncharted for me, broadening my horizons. I really want to make the most of it.
Perfect. Let us map out the first three steps, so the aspiration becomes attainable.
Yes! With a clear goal on the horizon, I am ready to seize the moment. This really feels like a fresh start.

So here is how I am thinking about the year ahead. This time next year, I will not be sitting at home wondering what to do. I will be travelling. I have set my sights on one clear goal: by next spring, I will have finally walked a long-overdue trail I have dreamed about for years. Everything else falls into place around that. I will be saving a little each month, I will be training on the weekends, and I will be planning the route step by step. It is not a vague aspiration anymore. It is a plan with a date. And honestly, I cannot wait to seize the moment and make the most of this fresh chapter.

People ask me what I will be doing in five years, and I always smile, because I genuinely do not know all the details. But I do know the direction. By then, I will have broadened my horizons in ways I cannot fully picture yet. I will be exploring places that are still uncharted for me, and I will have ticked off at least three goals I keep postponing. The trick, I have learned, is to map out the big destinations and stay flexible about everything else. A fresh start does not need a perfect plan. It just needs one clear goal on the horizon and the courage to begin.
